text:
In the first scene, I feel slightly overwhelmed by the closeness of bodies and the casual hubbub of social gathering. There is an energy of anticipation, as if something is about to begin or has just ended. I feel aware of my own physical presence, awkward and alert in the crowd.

In the second scene, the environment feels more cerebral, reflective, wrapped in the language of innovation and research. The swirling colors and illuminated words engage my curiosity while also evoking a sense of ambiguity. I am prompted to ponder clarity, perception, and the limited nature of my own understanding. Here, I am more at ease—drawn into thought and a sense of quiet wonder.
